rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT is continued to September 17, 2021 pending the disposition of the California Supreme Court Request for Review. Outstanding discovery order compliance is stayed until September 17, 2021. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT BELL SOUND USA LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. In its ruling on the prior demurrer the Court instructed the Plaintiff to allege specific facts (argument, case citations and allegations "on information and belief" without the facts giving rise to such belief are not factual allegations). Plaintiff did not do so. Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to allege a viable cause of action for interference with contractual relations, including alleging facts (not conclusions) of Defendant's knowledge of the existence and the terms of the contract; facts addressing statute of frauds; and facts addressing fair competition privilege. The Court notes that neither the statute of frauds not the fair competition issues raised in the demurrer were addressed by Plaintiff in opposition. All other causes of action/remedies asserted against this defendant are derivative of the interference with contractual relations. They should only be re-alleged if defendant can in good faith allege a cause of action for interference with contractual relations. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANTS CIM GROUP, L. P.'S AND 246 FIRST STREET, (SF) OWNER, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. In its ruling on the prior demurrer the Court instructed the Plaintiff to allege specific facts (argument, case citations and allegations "on information and belief" without the facts giving rise to such belief are not factual allegations). Plaintiff did not do so. Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, to support each element of each contract-based and promissory estoppel causes of action. Plaintiff shall also allege facts addressing the Statute of Frauds. Plaintiff shall allege fraud with requisite particularity. As to all other causes of action and/or remedies, they depend on viability of contract-based and fraud causes of action and shall be re-alleged only if those causes of action are properly asserted. The Court will not consider Ng Declaration and Exhibit(s) thereto for the purpose of this demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: AEGIS ASSET MANAGEMENT LLC VS. CBRE CAPITAL MARKETS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT CBRE, INC., ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CBRE CAPITAL'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED. In its ruling on the prior demurrer the Court instructed the Plaintiff to allege specific facts (argument, case citations and allegations "on information and belief" without the facts giving rise to such belief are not factual allegations). Plaintiff did not do so. Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to allege in good faith all elements of breach of contract with this defendant, i.e. contract formation and breach of specific terms (whether independent contract or "multi-party" contract Plaintiff to allege each element as to this defendant). See Warington Lumber Co. v. Fullerton Mortg. & Escrow Co. (1963) 222 Cal. App. 2d 706. As to the promissory estoppel cause of action, Plaintiff to allege each element of this cause of action as to this Defendant. Plaintiff shall further allege fraud with particularity as to this Defendant. For declaratory relief cause of action, to the extent it may be based on claims to property, Plaintiff to allege this Defendant's interest in the property. As to all other causes of action and/or remedies, they depend on viability of contract-based and fraud causes of action and shall be re-alleged only if those causes of action are properly asserted. All new allegations to be in bold font. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JOHN KOURY VS. MATTHEW GRAHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2021 line 7. DEFENDANT MATTHEW GRAHAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff, Koury, as successor trustee of the owner Trust; OR for Plaintiff Koury, as manager with written authority to recover possession provided by the Trust, to state a claim as a real party in interest. Plaintiffs cite no authority for the proposition that owner and manager may simultaneously have standing and be real parties in interest seeking to recover possession in an unlawful detainer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: POLK STREET ASSOCIATES VS. BERBER GROUP, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 10, 2021 line 8. DEFENDANT BERBER GROUP, LLC'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. Complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that Plaintiff is required to address the moratorium in order to state a cause of action. Defendant may raise the moratorium, and any facts establishing its applicability to the defendant, as an affirmative defense. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
